preamble: The shift from the lateral [l] to the voiced retroflex fricative [ʐ] can occur as a fortition or as a hypercorrection in speakers who normally merge these sounds.

examples.note: Hypercorrection in attempts to correctly place the 'r' sound.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
